triage.obstruction: The Riemann Hypothesis is an open problem; no known proof exists. Finite zero-checking is a numerical verification, not a proof of the hypothesis.
The general n-body (n>=3) problem has no closed-form solution; only special solutions (restricted problem, Lagrange points, Jacobi integral) exist.
No dimensions, coordinates, or any other information about the triangle are provided, making the problem unsolvable.
